body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

The scenario's three Expected variants are: (1) clean exit, (2) SIGKILL while idle in ready, and (3) SIGKILL mid-turn while still working. For the codex adapter, irrlicht observes variant 1 cleanly — the rollout-keyed session is born in ready, the turn completes, and process_exited fires after the working window closes. Variants 2 and 3 are NOT currently observable end-to-end because codex's headless `codex exec` is single-shot (every invocation IS variant 1) and the codex interactive driver does not currently expose `sigkill` / `restart` / `exit_clean` step types — claudecode's interactive driver is the only one that does.

Both invariants asserted by variant 1 hold in the recording: process_exited fires within ~4.4 s of session birth, and no transition to waiting appears anywhere in the window.
caveats: Variants 2 and 3 unobservable: would need a driver-level sigkill primitive added to drive-codex-interactive.sh (similar to drive-claudecode-interactive.sh's step_sigkill).

The recording does not show explicit working→ready transitions for the rollout session because codex's reply was extremely short (one token) and may have completed inside a single classifier tick. The session_birth phase still passes because the proc-<PID> pre-session row covers session birth, and the v1_exit phase still passes because process_exited fires cleanly.

sources.note: Confirmed only send/wait_turn/sleep/interrupt step types are handled.
